message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "claim with enchantment 9 sword, damage 1000 → payout 400"
Current error: Expected payout 400 remainingCap 1600, got 900/1100
Passing tests so far: 19

Per MHPCO rules: damage to items with enchantment level ≥ 8 is reimbursed at 50% of the damage amount.

Sword (enchantment 9), damage 1000: reimbursable = 500, then deductible 100 → payout 400. Cap = 2000, remainingCap = 1600.

Add the high-enchantment claim rule. When the damaged item (looked up from the policy by itemType) has enchantment >= 8, reimburse 50% then deduct.

Keep all 19 previously passing tests green. Test 18 has enchantment 3 (< 8), Test 19 has enchantment 5 (< 8). They should be unaffected.

Run `pnpm test`.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal change needed.
Now I understand. I need to add the high-enchantment claim rule. The damage lookup needs to find the item in the policy by itemType and check its enchantment level.

content.text:
All 20 tests pass (19 previously passing + 1 new).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added high-enchantment claim rule - when the damaged item (looked up from policy by itemType) has enchantment >= 8, the reimbursable amount is 50% of damage before deductible.
- Result: All 20 tests now pass.
- Approach: Minimal addition - introduced `reimbursableAmount` helper that returns 50% for high-enchantment items (>= 8), else full damage. The deductible is applied after. Lookup by itemType in `policy.items` finds the item to check its enchantment.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_23-33-33_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
